steps.title: Use the systematic finding
steps.content: The Mongolia-Cameroon comparison led some people to speculate that democratic institutions weaken inflation control. But Mehta et al. found that democratic strength, when paired with low inequality, restrains inflationary pressures.

steps.title: Reject unsupported explanations
steps.content: Choice B invents measurement error that exaggerates inflation in democratic countries. Choice C ignores the finding that democratic strength matters when associated with low inequality. Choice D invents a problem with the international indices. The answer is A.
